channel.title: A Cardinal's Wings
channel.description: Recent content on A Cardinal's Wings

item.title: 1.1 Changing our POV
item.description:
Changing Your POV#

“Unless you make the subconscious conscious, it will direct your life and you will call it fate.”
— Carl Jung

TLDR#

Shifting your POV — what Justin calls paradigm shifting, or PS — is very difficult, but it is probably the most effective way to change yourself, and maybe others.

Our paradigms shape our attitude and behaviors, which shape our actions.

One common application of this is if you see everybody in your class as an opponent, you simply won’t see opportunities that could benefit everybody — especially when that would actually put you in a better spot than your solo grinding!

item.title: 4.1 Bingeing? Scrolling Too Much?
item.description:
4.1 Bingeing? Scrolling Too Much? Solutions!#

“In the throes of desire, there’s no deciding. Binding ourselves is a way to be free from temptation.”

— Anna Lembke

TLDR#

If you want to stop bingeing, do not just fight cravings in the moment.

Instead, understand the pleasure-pain cycle, accept some boredom, and build systems that protect you before desire takes over.

The Problem#

“Oh I… just scrolled five hours of YouTube! AHH… I feel like a loser! Like bro, it’s not even fun after the third hour, and yet I still do this!”

  • Ending and Final Thoughts
  • Sources

item.title: 1.2 How to receive advice?
item.description:
How Do We Get New Maps?#

TLDR#

Maps, or paradigms, are powerful.

Good information shows us a more accurate map.

We can build better maps by having good connections with people and reading good books, among other ways.

Above all: use what we learn.

We shouldn’t hoard knowledge like we’re in a zombie apocalypse, because thoughts unrealized are wasted.

It’s incredibly difficult to understand something without actually using it, because life is often smarter than our ruminations.

item.title: 2.1 Afraid? --> Shift POV and just do something!
item.description:
Afraid? Shift POV and Just Do Something!#

TLDR#

The problem: we hesitate when faced with the opportunity to grow.

This fear hurts us because we may end up missing the opportunity, along with the many benefits it could’ve brought us.

So how should we deal with this?

A solution: shift your view.

Use a new map:

  • What’s true is that you’re excited. Your body can’t tell the difference for some reason.
  • Nobody cares as much as you think.
  • It’s natural to be scared of new things.
  • We always have the choice to ignore this caveman brain’s fight-or-flight reaction.

Yeah, our bodies lie to us sometimes.

item.title: 4.2 Building and Breaking Habits
item.description:
4.2 Building and Breaking Habits#

(Note)

This section is based on what I learned from Atomic Habits.

I see this section as a generalized version of Chapter 4.1. I wrote about dealing with scrolling first because I thought it would be more useful.

Anyways…

TLDR#

The repetition of behavior, not the sincerity of belief, is what shapes character.

You become the kind of person who does the things you repeatedly do.
